rulings.Case Title: MAX SHIRRY-WYSS VS. NOB HILL PLACE PARTNERS LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 29, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT NOB HILL PLACE PARTNERS LP's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Nob Hill Place Partners, LP's Motion for Determination of Good Faith Settlement is GRANTED. Defendant Nob Hill Partners, LP demonstrated that under the Tech-Bilt factors, the parties' settlement was made in good faith pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6. = (501/CFH)

All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY Z DILLEY ET AL VS. BURRADELL CONSTRUCTION INC. A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 29, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANTs BURRADELL CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA and ALAN BURRADELL's Motion For Summary Judgment Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant Burradell's Motion for Summary Judgment. is GRANTED. Plaintiffs failed to present competent admissible evidence establishing a triable issue of material fact related to the personal liability of Defendant Alan Burradell under California Code of Civil Procedure section 437(c)(p)(2). Plaintiffs presented evidence of a triable issue of material facts related to Defendant Burradell Construction, however, did not present responsive evidence of Defendant Alan Burradell's personal liability.= (501/CFH)
